reasoning: The question asks if there will be more than 10 times the baseline number of 'Battles' in Argentina during the 30 days before the resolution date. The baseline is 'one plus the 30-day average over the previous 360 days,' and currently stands at exactly 1.000. This indicates there were 0 'Battles' in Argentina over the past year according to ACLED data. For the condition to be met, Argentina would need to experience at least 11 'Battles' (which require violent interaction between two organized armed groups) in a single 30-day period. In Argentina, clashes between protesters and police are coded as 'Riots' or 'Protests', not 'Battles'. Drug violence in Rosario has not generated events classified as 'Battles' over the past year either. To go from zero 'Battles' annually to 11+ within a month would require an unprecedented surge in armed conflict or civil war, which is extremely unlikely by early 2026. Therefore, I place this at the minimum allowable probability of 2%.
